data.narrative:
Hadean is a Series B–stage defense and security technology company that has raised $40M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms operating in the same sector and stage, including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using $40M in total raised as a proxy for current implied value, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $76M. Peer-median analysis examines the 6 most comparable same-sector, same-stage private companies. The median valuation across this set is $1.2B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.5B. Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, and Harmattan AI anchor the upper end at $1.4B–$1.5B; Castelion and Defense Unicorns sit at the $1.0B floor.

The wide spread between methods reflects material uncertainty about Hadean's competitive positioning relative to better-capitalized peers. The triangulated range spans $65M (floor) to $1.4B (ceiling), with a median of $638M. The stage-progression result sits well below peer comparables, suggesting either that Hadean's raised capital substantially understates enterprise value or that it commands a meaningful discount to the peer set. Resolution of this gap requires closer examination of revenue traction, customer concentration, and product differentiation relative to the identified comparables.
